name: Monotone Constraint Gradient Boosting — Physics-Informed Tree Splits
technique: Apply monotone_constraints to ~20 directionally-certain features in XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost fleet models. Monotone constraints prevent tree splits that would produce physically impossible relationships (e.g., higher net rating → lower win probability). In high-dimensional spaces (200 features post-GA), models frequently learn spurious inversions from training noise. Monotone constraints eliminate this variance source without increasing bias for constrained features, directly improving Brier score via the resolution decomposition term.

source_papers.title: Monotonicity Constraints in Multiclass XGBoost
source_papers.key_finding: Monotone constraints in XGBoost via one-vs-rest decomposition maintain interpretable feature-prediction relationships and reduce overfitting. Applicable to multiclass and binary settings. 2025 validation of the approach.

source_papers.title: A Better Method to Enforce Monotonic Constraints in Regression and Classification Trees
source_papers.key_finding: Improved monotone enforcement outperforms LightGBM's built-in monotone_constraints at similar compute. Direct application: use this method as post-processing validation on top of LightGBM monotone output.

source_papers.title: arXiv:2503.15177 — Monotone Constraints in ML Models (March 2025)
source_papers.key_finding: Recent 2025 validation of monotone constraint ML across tabular settings. Confirms improved generalization and calibration when domain knowledge encodes directional relationships.

why_novel: None of P001-P006 or the existing .md proposals address the source of resolution errors in tree models: spurious non-monotone feature-outcome relationships. Monotone constraints are a training-time structural fix — orthogonal to calibration (P002, P006), to features (P003, P004), and to model architecture (P001, P005). They reduce the hypothesis space using domain knowledge that is free and always correct.
brier_decomposition_target: Targets resolution term. Spurious inversions — where the model incorrectly assigns lower win probability to teams with higher net rating — inflate the resolution component. Constraining ~20 features reduces tree variance without touching the remaining ~180 unconstrained features.
implementation_steps: Step 1: Audit 200-feature space — identify features from directionally_certain_features list that survived GA selection (expected ~8-12 per space)
Step 2: XGBoost — add monotone_constraints dict to xgb.train() params: {feature_idx: +1 or -1}
Step 3: LightGBM — add 'monotone_constraints': [0, +1, -1, ...] list matching feature column order
Step 4: CatBoost — add monotone_constraints=[(feature_name, 1)] list to CatBoostClassifier()
Step 5: Run GA evolution for 5 generations with constrained models — compare Brier to unconstrained baseline
Step 6: If Brier improves, add monotone constraint dict to all 6 HF island model configs
Step 7: Validate: check that constrained model's net_rating_diff feature is actually monotone in SHAP partial dependence plot

implementation_code_sketch.catboost:
mono_list = [(f, d) for f, d in directional_map.items() if f in feature_cols]
CatBoostClassifier(monotone_constraints=mono_list)

complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P002_venn_abers: Orthogonal: P002 fixes marginal calibration post-hoc. P007 prevents resolution errors at training time. Stack: train with P007 constraints → apply P002 calibration.
complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P006_mcboost: Additive: MCBoost corrects subgroup calibration after training. P007 prevents systematic resolution errors during training. Both reduce Brier via different decomposition components.
complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P003_fatigue: Monotone constraint on rest_disadvantage features amplifies P003's signal by preventing the model from learning anti-correlated noise on those same features.
complementarity_with_existing_proposals.P001_tabicl: TabICLv2 is a separate model architecture — P007 applies to XGBoost/CatBoost/LightGBM fleet. Parallel tracks.

evidence: SJMD-2025: Monotone XGBoost consistently outperforms unconstrained XGBoost on structured prediction when domain constraints are valid.
arXiv:2011.00986: Improved monotone tree method reduces test error vs unconstrained by 3-8% on benchmark tasks.

NBA domain: net_rating_diff is the strongest predictor (SHAP, PMC 2024). Adding monotone constraint prevents gradient boosting from assigning negative marginal contributions to this feature in edge cases.

High-dimensional tabular overfitting: with 200 features and ~9000 games, many spurious inversions appear in training. Constraining the ~10 most directionally certain features eliminates the worst overfitting targets.
2025 validation: Kaggle discussion confirms monotone_constraints in XGBoost/LGBM sports models improves OOF Brier by 0.001-0.003 for high-dimensional feature sets.
risk: Low-Medium. Risk: if any 'directionally certain' feature is NOT monotone in truth (e.g., too many rest days = player rustiness), the constraint harms performance. Mitigation: validate each constraint via partial dependence plot before applying. Only constrain features where SHAP partial dependence is already monotone in unconstrained model.
quick_win_path: 3 hours: 1h — identify which directional features are in current 200-feature GA winner spaces. 1h — add constraint dicts to XGBoost + LightGBM training cells. 1h — run 5-generation GA evolution comparison and measure Brier delta.
fleet_scope: Applies to all 6 HF islands (XGBoost + LightGBM + CatBoost variants). Single config change per model type propagates to entire fleet.
